summary: High‑energy ’73 show with explosive He’s Gone > Truckin’ > Other One sequence
blurb: The second set builds nonstop momentum, highlighted by a scorching He’s Gone → Truckin’ → The Other One → Spanish Jam run. First‑set gems like Box of Rain and Playing In The Band showcase tight playing and Jerry’s top‑form solos. Overall the show is a solid example of ’73 firepower.
review: On March 31, 1973 at Buffalo’s War Memorial Auditorium the Dead delivered one of their classic early‑’70s outings. A crisp soundboard capture reveals a first set that swings through Mississippi Half‑Step Uptown Toodeloo, Box of Rain and Tennessee Jed with tight ensemble work, while Playing In The Band teases the audience for what’s to come. The second set erupts into an unforgettable marathon: He’s Gone launches directly into Truckin’, which then rockets into a ferocious Other One that spills over into Spanish Jam before landing on a soaring I Know You Rider. Highlights also include a rousing Bertha → Greatest Story Ever Told segue, the rock‑steady Promised Land and a blistering El Paso. Keith Godchaux’s keys sit nicely in the mix, Bill Kreutzmann drives the jams with his powerful snare and tom work, and Donna’s backing vocals add color without overwhelming the band. All told, this show captures the Dead at a peak of improvisational daring and collective chemistry.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: All three recordings are marked positive; reviewers repeatedly praise the He’s Gone → Truckin’ → Other One sequence, Jerry’s top‑form solos and the tight rhythm section. No consistent negative comments appear, so a strong positive adjustment is applied.

best_recording.reason: Highest audio fidelity (good‑to‑excellent SBD), captures the full jam flow without dropouts, and is widely cited as the clearest representation of this show.

key_highlights: Explosive He’s Gone → Truckin’ → The Other One → Spanish Jam sequence drives the second set.
Tight first‑set playing with Box of Rain, Tennessee Jed and a soaring Playing In The Band.
Keith Godchaux’s keyboards are nicely balanced, adding depth to the improvisations.
Bill Kreutzmann’s powerful drumming and brief but effective drum solo underpin the jams.

band_performance.Jerry: Tone on point and in top form; spectacular, frantic solos on Tennessee Jed and throughout the second‑set jams.
band_performance.Phil: Bass tears it up, laying a solid foundation for the long improvisations and adding baritone vocals on He’s Gone.
band_performance.Bob: Solid rhythm work with occasional highlighted figure; no musical criticism noted across recordings.
band_performance.Keith: Keyboards sit nicely in the mix, providing depth especially during the second‑set peaks.
band_performance.Billy: Excellent drumming with heavy snare and tom presence; brief drum solo is punchy and drives the jams.
